Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2025-03-05 section=business period_end=2025-01-31
ITEM 1. BUSINESS
Overview
Salesforce, Inc. ("Salesforce," the "Company," "we" or
…[truncated 21937 chars]…
tunity, provides a pathway to effectively monetize the acquired products and drive significant operational efficiencies and presents a clear timeline for value accretion. Our acquisitions can range in size and complexity, from those that enhance or complement existing products and accelerate development of features to large-scale acquisitions that result in new service offerings. Our goal is to prioritize the use of our balance sheet, through cash and debt, to complete acquisitions.
We also manage a portfolio of strategic investments in both privately held and publicly traded companies focused primarily on enterprise cloud companies, technology startups and SIs. Our investments range from early to late stage companies, including investments made concurrent with a company's initial public offering. We invest in companies that we believe are digitally transforming their industries, improving customer experiences, helping us expand our solution ecosystem or supporting other corporate initiatives, including AI. We plan to continue making these types of strategic investments as opportunities arise that we find attractive, including investments in companies representing targeted businesses, technological initiatives and geographies. Our strategy includes growing our strategic investment portfolio, in part, by reinvesting proceeds from the sales of strategic investments.
7
Table of Contents
Technology, Development and Operations
We primarily deliver our solutions as highly scalable cloud computing application and platform services on a multi-tenant technology architecture. Multi-tenancy is an architectural approach that allows us to operate a single application instance for multiple organizations, treating all customers as separate tenants who run in virtual isolation from each other. This approach allows us to spread the cost of delivering our services across our user base and scale our business faster than traditional software vendors while focusing our resources on building new functionality and enhancing existing offerings.
We provide our services through cloud computing platform partners who offer Infrastructure-as-a-Service, including servers, storage, databases and networking, as well as through infrastructure designed and operated by us but secured within third-party data center facilities. We continue to invest and expand the deployment of Hyperforce, which allows our platform and applications to be delivered rapidly and reliably to locations worldwide and provides our customers autonomy and control over data residency.
Our technology and product efforts are focused on improving and enhancing the features, functionality, performance, availability and security of our existing service offerings, as well as developing new features, functionality and services. We also remain focused on integrating businesses, services and technologies from acquisitions. Performance, functional depth, security, usability, ease of integration and configuration and sustainability of our solutions influence our technology decisions and product direction.
Competition
The market for our service offerings is highly competitive, rapidly evolving and fragmented, and subject to changing technology with low barriers to entry, shifting customer needs and frequent introductions of new products and services.
Our current competitors include:
- vendors of packaged business software, as well as companies offering enterprise applications delivered through on-premises offerings from enterprise software application vendors and cloud computing application service providers, either individually or with others;
- software companies that provide their product or service free of charge as a single product or when bundled with other offerings, or only charge a premium for advanced features and functionality, as well as companies that offer solutions that are sold without a direct sales organization;
- vendors who offer software tailored to specific services, industries or market segments, as opposed to our full suite of service offerings including suppliers of traditional business intelligence and data preparation products, integration software vendors, marketing vendors, e-commerce solutions vendors, or AI software and service vendors;
- productivity tool and email providers, unified communications providers and consumer application companies that have entered the business software market; and
- traditional platform development environment companies and cloud computing development platform companies who may develop toolsets and products that allow customers to build new applications, including AI augmented applications, that run on the customers' current infrastructure or as hosted services, as well as would-be customers who may develop enterprise applications for internal use.
We believe more companies may become competitive threats due to the attractiveness of the markets in which we operate. We also expect our competition to change and evolve as we expand into more markets, with new offerings.
Customers
We sell to businesses of all sizes and in almost every industry worldwide. The number of paying subscriptions at each of our customers ranges from one to hundreds of thousands. None of our customers accounted for more than ten percent of our revenues in fiscal years 2025, 2024 or 2023. In addition, we do not have any material dependencies on any specific product, service or particular group or groups.
Customer Service and Support
We offer professional services to help customers achieve business results faster with Salesforce solutions. Our architects and innovation program teams act as advisors to plan and execute digital transformations for our customers. This includes implementation services for multi-offering and complex deployments. We provide best-practices and AI-based recommendations and adoption programs globally. In addition, we provide advanced education, including in-person and online courses, to certify our customers and partners on architecting, administering, deploying and developing our service offerings.
Our global customer support group responds to both business and technical inquiries about the use of our products via the web, telephone, email, social networks and other channels. We provide standard customer support during regular business hours to customers as part of our paying subscription editions. We also offer premier customer support that is either included in a premium success offering or sold for an additional fee, which can include services such as priority access to technical resources, developer support and system administration. In addition, we offer a premier priority support add-on that includes proactive monitoring, rapid incident response and instruction from a dedicated support team knowledgeable about the customer's specific
8
Table of Contents
enterprise architecture. With the launch of Agentforce, we've made it easier for customers to find answers by providing an even more intuitive experience on our Salesforce Help page. Agentforce can resolve customer issues swiftly and accurately because answers are grounded in our trusted unstructured content unified in Data Cloud. By automating simple and repetitive tasks, our support engineers will be able to augment and scale their time in a more valuable way.
Sales and Marketing
We sell our services primarily through our direct sales force, which comprises sales personnel based in regional hubs, field sales personnel based in territories close to their customers and self-service offerings.
To a lesser extent, we also utilize a network of partners who refer sales leads to us and assist in selling to prospects. This network includes global consulting firms, SIs and other partners. **Quantitative Equity Report: CRM (as of 2025-09-30)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** Quarterly revenue grew from $8.72B (Q3 2023) to $10.24B (Q2 2025). The latest quarter (Q2 2025) showed QoQ revenue growth of +4.1% and YoY growth of +9.8%. Diluted EPS accelerated, showing QoQ growth of +23.3% and YoY growth of +33.3%, reaching $1.96.
*   **Margins:** The latest gross margin was 77.2%, up from 75.4% a year prior (implied from Q2 2024 gross profit $7.17B / revenue $9.32B). The latest operating margin was 19.0%. Net margin expanded significantly to 16.4% in the latest period, up from 12.1% in Q4 2024.
*   **Cash-Flow:** **No cash-flow statement data provided for the lookback period.** TTM free cash flow is unavailable.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total assets were $97.57B with total equity of $61.33B. Total debt was stable at $8.44B. The debt-to-equity ratio is 0.138.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** The primary earnings quality indicator available is margin. The high and expanding gross margin (77.2%) and net margin (16.4%) suggest strong pricing power and operational leverage. However, the absence of cash-flow data precludes an assessment of cash conversion quality.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The capital structure is conservative with debt-to-equity of 0.138. Total equity has been stable around $58B-$61B over the period, showing a solid foundation.

**3. Valuation vs. Supplied History**
*   The latest PE (diluted) is 37.26. The provided one-year history shows a PE range of 36.4 to 77.7, placing the current valuation at the low end of its recent range.
*   The latest Price-to-Book is 3.774, within its one-year range of 3.7 to 6.1 and also near the low end.
*   The valuation compression from a PE of 61.32 (Oct 2024) to 37.26 (Sep 2025) coincides with the expansion in net margin from 12.1% to 16.4%.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
*   **Price Level:** The stock closed at $237.00 on 2025-09-30. The 365-day range is $226.48 to $369.00. The current price is 35.8% below the 52-week high.
*   **Return Context:** The stock has declined -12.8% over the most recent quarter and -14.1% over the last year. This negative price momentum contrasts with the positive fundamental trajectory of accelerating earnings and expanding margins. The quarterly return path shows significant volatility: +23.9% (Q4 2024), -21.5% (Q1 2025), +1.3% (Q2 2025), -12.8% (latest).

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** The combination of accelerating earnings growth (+33.3% YoY EPS) and a PE multiple at the low end of its historical range (37.26 vs. 77.7 high) presents a potential re-rating opportunity if growth sustains. The expanding net margin from 12.1% to 16.4% directly supported the earnings surge.
*   **Downside:** The severe lack of cash-flow data is a fundamental blind spot for assessing earnings quality. The stock has exhibited high price volatility, and the substantial decline from the $369 high indicates sensitivity to sentiment shifts. Valuation, while lower, remains elevated in absolute terms (PE 37.26, earnings yield 2.7%).

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
Based solely on the supplied numbers, the case presents a positive divergence between fundamental trajectory (strong earnings growth, margin expansion) and valuation reset (PE at range low). The key risk is the incomplete picture due to missing cash-flow data. The quantitative evidence leans cautiously positive, contingent on the durability of the earnings improvement demonstrated in the income statements.
CRM.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Salesforce (CRM)**
**Decision Date: 2025-10-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 10-K, 2025-03-05)**
Salesforce is a global leader in customer relationship management (CRM), providing a deeply unified, AI-powered platform. Its core franchise is built on connecting customer data to create a single source of truth, enabling personalized engagement. Key offerings include Sales, Service, Platform, Slack, Marketing, Commerce, and Analytics (Tableau). A central strategic pillar is **Agentforce**, an agentic layer for deploying autonomous AI agents across business functions to drive productivity and efficiency. The company's growth strategy focuses on cross-selling to existing customers, geographic expansion, industry-specific vertical solutions, and leveraging its partner ecosystem. The business model is primarily subscription-based.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Evidence)**
*   **Corporate Action / New Business Unit (2025-09-30):** Salesforce launched **Missionforce**, a new business unit targeting national security, positioning it to compete directly with companies like Palantir in the government and data analytics sector.
*   **Strategic Partnership / Geographic Expansion (2025-09-24 & 2025-09-23):** Youxin Technology acquired a 51% stake in Celnet Technology, a leading Salesforce partner in China, to enhance digital transformation and AI-driven CRM capabilities in that market.
*   **Capital Allocation (2025-09-17):** Salesforce was noted as one of three tech companies that announced significant share buyback programs, signaling financial strength.
*   **Market Sentiment (2025-09-25):** Despite recent analyst downgrades, Salesforce was identified alongside CrowdStrike and Fortinet as a stock with potential for market recovery due to strong institutional support and AI positioning.
*   **Competitive Landscape (2025-09-26):** A competitor, SayVo AI, unveiled advanced AI sales representatives with enterprise-grade scalability and seamless CRM integration, indicating ongoing competitive pressure in the AI sales tools space.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Legal and Reputational Exposure from Data Breaches.** Multiple class-action lawsuits (2025-09-08, 2025-09-09, 2025-09-10, 2025-09-25) allege Salesforce failed to adequately secure systems, leading to customer data breaches. A multidistrict litigation (MDL) is being sought (2025-09-10). This poses a direct risk to the company's core value of "trust" and could result in financial liabilities and reputational damage.
*   **Offset:** The company's 10-K (2025-03-05) explicitly states that "trust" is paramount and grounded in legal and regulatory frameworks for security and privacy. The ongoing lawsuits test this commitment.
*   **Risk: Intense and Evolving Competition.** The 10-K (2025-03-05) describes the market as "highly competitive, rapidly evolving and fragmented" with low barriers to entry. Recent news shows new AI-native competitors (e.g., SayVo AI, 2025-09-26) and expansion by large incumbents (e.g., Oracle's cloud growth, 2025-09-18).
*   **Offset:** The company's strategy (10-K, 2025-03-05) emphasizes its deeply unified platform, extensive ecosystem, and the launch of Agentforce as key differentiators. The launch of Missionforce (2025-09-30) is an offensive move to capture a new, high-value market.
*   **Risk: Execution on AI Strategy.** The company's narrative is heavily centered on AI and Agentforce. Success depends on customer adoption and the ability to monetize these new offerings effectively.
*   **Offset:** The 10-K (2025-03-05) details a broad suite of AI-integrated products and a strategy focused on driving multi-product adoption. Web context (2025-08-17, 2025-08-28) notes efficient scaling and durable fundamentals, suggesting a capacity to invest in this transition.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral to Slightly Positive.** The franchise remains strong, defined by a market-leading, unified CRM platform with a clear and ambitious AI strategy (Agentforce, Missionforce). Recent developments show proactive capital allocation and strategic expansion. However, this positive view is materially tempered by significant and escalating legal risks surrounding data security, which directly challenge the company's foundational value of trust. The competitive landscape remains fierce. The qualitative outlook hinges on the company's ability to navigate the legal overhang while successfully executing its AI-driven growth strategy.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'The latest operating margin was 19.0%.' The raw evidence confirms operating_margin=0.19, so this is consistent. However, the report also states 'Net margin expanded significantly to 16.4% in the latest period, up from 12.1% in Q4 2024.' The raw evidence shows net_margin=0.164 at the latest point and net_margin=0.121 at 2024-10-01 (which corresponds to Q3 2024 data, not Q4 2024). The 12.1% net margin is associated with the 2024-10-01 valuation snapshot, which uses Q3 2024 period-end data (2024-10-31). The report's reference to 'Q4 2024' is imprecise but not materially misleading since the net margin figure itself is correct.
corrections.required_action: Minor clarification: the 12.1% net margin corresponds to the Q3 2024 period (filing 2024-10-31), not Q4 2024. No change to conclusion needed.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'The provided one-year history shows a PE range of 36.4 to 77.7, placing the current valuation at the low end of its recent range.' The raw evidence confirms pe_diluted range 36.4..77.7 and the latest value of 37.264. This is accurate. However, the report also says 'The valuation compression from a PE of 61.32 (Oct 2024) to 37.26 (Sep 2025) coincides with the expansion in net margin from 12.1% to 16.4%.' The raw evidence shows the PE at 2024-10-01 was 61.321 and net_margin was 0.121, and at 2025-09-30 PE was 37.264 and net_margin was 0.164. This is consistent. The claim is supported.
corrections.required_action: No action needed; claim verified against raw evidence.

corrections.issue: The qual report references 'Web context (2025-08-17, 2025-08-28) notes efficient scaling and durable fundamentals, suggesting a capacity to invest in this transition.' The raw qualitative evidence includes a 2025-08-17 article mentioning 'efficient scaling—driven by AI adoption and consumption-based pricing—ensures sustainable cash flow' and a 2025-08-28 article mentioning '12.9% year-over-year increase in Platform and Other revenue and a 10% year-over-year rise in operating cash flow.' The qual report's characterization is fair but slightly vague. The 10% YoY rise in operating cash flow cited in the web context is notable because the quant report repeatedly flags the absence of cash-flow data as a blind spot. This is a minor inconsistency in emphasis but not a factual conflict since the web context is secondary/opinion source material.
corrections.required_action: No action needed; the web context is clearly labeled as secondary and the quant report's cash-flow caveat remains valid for the primary data pack.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: CRM presents a classic value trap vs. value opportunity debate: accelerating earnings (+33% YoY EPS) and a PE at the low end of its historical range (37x vs. 77x high) are offset by significant legal overhang from data breach lawsuits, missing cash-flow validation, and a stock that has already de-rated sharply (-14% over the past year). The risk/reward is roughly balanced with a mild positive lean.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: CRM presents a classic value trap vs. value opportunity debate: accelerating earnings (+33% YoY EPS) and a PE at the low end of its historical range (37x vs. 77x high) are offset by significant legal overhang from data breach lawsuits, missing cash-flow validation, and a stock that has already de-rated sharply (-14% over the past year). The risk/reward is roughly balanced with a mild positive lean.
key_factors: Earnings acceleration with PE at historical low: Q2 2025 EPS grew +33.3% YoY to $1.96 while the PE compressed to 37.26, near the bottom of its 36.4-77.7 one-year range, creating a potential re-rating setup if growth sustains.

Margin expansion validates profitability inflection: Net margin expanded from 12.1% (Q3 2024) to 16.4% (Q2 2025), and gross margin improved to 77.2%, demonstrating durable operational leverage beyond cost-cutting.

Legal and reputational risk from data breach litigation: Multiple class-action lawsuits filed in September 2025 with an MDL being sought, directly challenging the company's core 'trust' value proposition and creating uncertain financial liability.

AI strategy execution remains unproven at scale: Agentforce and Missionforce represent credible catalysts, but competitive pressure from AI-native entrants (e.g., SayVo AI) and the absence of cash-flow data to validate earnings quality leave the thesis incomplete.

Item 1.    Business
General
Verizon Communications Inc. (the Company) is a holding company that, acting through its subsidiaries (together with the Company, collectively, Verizon), is one of the world's leading providers of communications, technology, information and streaming products and services to consumers, businesses and gov
…[truncated 21685 chars]…
es, we have entered into intra-market spectrum swaps designed to increase the amount of contiguous spectrum within frequency bands in a specific market. Contiguous spectrum improves network performance and efficiency. These swaps, as well as any spectrum purchases, require us to obtain governmental approvals.
Information regarding spectrum license transactions, including our pending acquisition of select spectrum licenses of United States Cellular Corporation and certain of its subsidiaries, is presented in Note 3 to the consolidated financial statements included in this Annual Report.
Competition and Related Trends
The telecommunications industry is highly competitive. The rapid development of new technologies, services and products has eliminated many of the distinctions among wireless, cable, internet and traditional telephone services and brought new competitors to our markets. We expect competition to remain intense as traditional and non-traditional participants seek increased market share.
Competitive factors within the telecommunications industry include, among others, network reliability, speed, capacity and coverage; pricing and promotional strategies; the quality of customer service; marketing, sales and distribution capabilities; development of new and enhanced products and services; the ability to anticipate and respond to various factors and trends affecting our industry; the availability of capital resources; and regulatory changes.
Competition remains intense as a result of various factors, including aggressive pricing, increased levels of promotions and service plan discounts, and offerings that include additional bundled premium content or other perks, in some cases specifically targeting Verizon customers. Competition may increase as smaller, stand-alone wireless service providers merge or transfer licenses to larger, better capitalized wireless service providers and as MVNOs resell wireless communication services. In addition, aggressive network deployment as well as increasing government incentives related to it may enhance the ability of certain of our competitors to compete with us.
With respect to our wireless connectivity products and services, we compete against other national wireless service providers, including AT&T Inc. and T-Mobile US, Inc., as well as various regional wireless service providers. We also compete for retail activations with resellers that buy bulk wholesale service from wireless service providers, including Verizon, and resell it to their customers. Resellers include cable companies, such as Comcast Corporation and Charter Communications, Inc., and others. Several major cable operators also offer bundles with wireless services through strategic relationships.
We also face competition from other communications and technology companies seeking to increase their brand recognition and capture market share with respect to the provision of wireless products and services, in addition to non-traditional offerings in mobile data. For example, Microsoft Corporation, Alphabet Inc., Apple Inc., Meta Platforms, Inc. and others are offering alternative means for messaging and making wireless voice calls that, in certain cases, can be used in lieu of the wireless providers' voice service, as well as alternative means of accessing video content. In addition, we are seeing increasing competition in the provisioning of internet access by low Earth orbit satellite companies as well in the area of fixed wireless offerings that provide an alternative to traditional landline internet service providers.
With respect to Fios and our other broadband services, we compete against cable companies, wireless service providers, domestic and foreign telecommunications providers, satellite television companies, low Earth orbit satellite companies, internet service providers, OTT providers and other companies that offer network services and managed enterprise solutions. We also
8
Table of Contents
face increasing competition from other internet portal providers. We expect the market will continue to shift from traditional linear video to OTT offerings. We also expect customer migration from traditional voice services to wireless services to continue.
Companies with a global presence are increasingly competing with us in our Business segment. A relatively small number of telecommunications and integrated service providers with global operations serve customers in the global enterprise market and, to a lesser extent, the global wholesale market. We compete with these providers for large contracts to provide integrated solutions to global enterprises and government customers. Many of these companies have strong market presence, brand recognition and existing customer relationships, all of which contribute to intense competition.
In the Enterprise and Public Sector markets, competition levels remain high, primarily as a result of increased industry focus on technology convergence. We compete in this area with system integrators, carriers, and hardware and software providers. In addition, some of the largest information technology services companies are making strategic acquisitions, divesting non-strategic assets and forging new alliances to improve their cost structure. Many new alliances and acquisitions have focused on developing fields, such as cloud computing, software defined networking, communication applications and other computing tasks via networks, rather than by the use of in-house machines.
In the Business Markets and Other category, customer purchasing behaviors and preferences continue to evolve. Solution speed and simplicity are becoming key differentiators for customers who are seeking full life-cycle offers that simplify the process of starting, running and growing their businesses. Mobile network operators and other carriers are aggressively targeting this customer group with a variety of bundles and discounts as it is among the largest and fastest growing categories in the Business segment.
Our Wholesale business competes with traditional carriers for metro/mid/long-haul fiber, voice and IP services. In addition, mobile video and data needs are driving a greater need for wireless backhaul. Network providers, cable companies and specialty firms are competitors for this business opportunity.
Regulatory Trends
Regulatory Landscape
Verizon operates in a regulated and highly competitive market, as described above. Some of our competitors are subject to fewer regulatory constraints than Verizon. For many services offered by Verizon, the FCC is our primary regulator. The FCC has jurisdiction over interstate telecommunications services and other matters under the Communications Act of 1934, as amended (Communications Act). Other Verizon services are subject to various state and local regulations.
Federal, State and Local Regulation
Wireless Services
The FCC regulates several aspects of our wireless operations. Generally, the FCC has jurisdiction over the construction, operation, acquisition and transfer of wireless communications systems. All wireless services require use of radio frequency spectrum, the assignment and distribution of which is subject to FCC oversight. If demand continues to increase or if new spectrum is required for a future generation of technology, we can meet our needs for licensed spectrum by purchasing licenses or leasing spectrum from others, or by participating in a competitive bidding process to acquire new spectrum from the FCC. Those processes are subject to certain reviews, approvals and potential conditions.
Today, Verizon holds FCC spectrum licenses that allow it to provide a wide range of mobile and fixed communications services, including both voice and data services. FCC spectrum licenses typically have a term of 10 years, at which time they are subject to renewal. While the FCC has routinely renewed all of Verizon's wireless licenses, challenges co

**VZ Quantitative Report – 2025-10-01 (Point-in-Time Evidence)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin & Balance Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue:** Latest Q2 2025 revenue was $34.50B. TTM revenue sums to $134.12B. YoY quarterly growth was +5.2%.
*   **Earnings:** Latest Q2 2025 net income was $5.12B (EPS $1.18). TTM net income is $18.22B. YoY EPS growth was +8.3%. A significant Q4 2023 net loss of -$2.57B (EPS -$0.65) is a notable outlier.
*   **Margins:** Latest operating margin is 16.3% (Q2 2025). Net margin is 7.6%. The Q4 2023 operating income collapsed to $0.60B, indicating a period of severely depressed profitability.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total equity grew to $104.36B (Q2 2025) from $93.80B (Q4 2023). Total liabilities were $278.92B, implying a debt/equity ratio of ~2.67. Total assets are stable around ~$380B.
*   **Cash Flow:** Cash flow statements are unavailable for the entire lookback period. Free cash flow generation is therefore not quantifiable from this data.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Self-Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** The large Q4 2023 loss (-$2.57B) followed by consistent positive earnings indicates episodic volatility. Net margin has stabilized at 7.6% over the last three quarters after the 0.1% margin in the loss quarter. The absence of cash flow data prevents assessment of accruals or cash conversion.
*   **Balance Sheet Resilience:** Leverage is high, with total liabilities of $278.92B against equity of $104.36B. The equity base has improved by +$10.56B from the Q4 2023 low of $93.80B, providing a modest strengthening trend.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
*   **PE (diluted):** 18.095 (range over past year: 14.9 to 19.1). Current PE is near the top of its one-year range.
*   **Price-to-Book:** 1.901 (range: 1.6 to 2.0). Current P/B is near the middle of its one-year range.
*   **Earnings Yield:** 0.055 (5.5%).
*   **Context:** The stock is not trading at a low multiple; its current PE is at the high end of its recent history. ROE has been stable at 0.105.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
*   **Latest Price:** $43.95 on 2025-09-30.
*   **52-Week Range:** Low $37.59, High $47.35. The current price is 7.2% below the high and 16.9% above the low.
*   **Returns:** The quarterly return (from 2025-07-01) was +0.6%. The annual return (from 2024-09-26) was -1.5%. The path included a sharp -10.4% decline in Q4 2024 and a +13.5% surge in Q1 2025.
*   **Fundamental vs. Price:** Annual price return of -1.5% contrasts with YoY EPS growth of +8.3%, suggesting the market has not rewarded recent earnings improvement. The price is closer to the lower end of its one-year range while valuation multiples are at the higher end.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** Consistent TTM net income of $18.22B and sequential revenue growth (+3.0% QoQ, +5.2% YoY) provide fundamental support. Equity has shown a clear recovery trend over seven quarters.
*   **Downside:** Very high financial leverage (liabilities/equity ~2.67x). The earnings history is volatile (Q4 2023 loss). Cash generation cannot be verified without cash flow data. Valuation multiples (PE=18.1) are at the upper bound of their recent range, offering limited expansion potential based on supplied history.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
Based on supplied point-in-time data, VZ presents a **neutral quantitative risk/reward profile**. The stock is not demonstrably cheap, trading at a PE (18.1) at the high end of its recent range despite an annual price decline. Fundamental earnings have shown recovery and growth (TTM net income $18.22B, YoY EPS +8.3%). However, balance sheet leverage is high (D/E ~2.67x), historical earnings volatility is significant, and the absence of cash flow data obscures a key quality metric. The price path has been volatile and failed to keep pace with recent earnings gains. The core tension is between improving recent profitability metrics and a valuation that does not screen as low, coupled with substantial leverage and unknown cash flow characteristics.
VZ.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ)**
**Date:** 2025-10-01

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Based on its 2024 10-K, Verizon is a leading global communications and technology company operating through two segments: Verizon Consumer Group (76% of 2024 revenue) and Verizon Business Group (22%). The Consumer segment provides wireless services to ~115 million retail connections and broadband services (Fios, FWA) to ~10 million connections. The Business segment provides similar connectivity and advanced services to enterprises, public sector, and wholesale customers. The company emphasizes its extensive 5G network, fiber infrastructure (including Fios), and spectrum portfolio as core competitive assets. A strategic agreement to acquire Frontier Communications was announced in September 2024 to enhance its fiber footprint.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Text)**
*   **Strategic & Corporate Actions:**
    *   **2025-09-30:** In talks to acquire wireless spectrum from EchoStar, part of the latter's monetization strategy.
    *   **2025-09-25:** Announced as the first carrier to offer the new AI-powered Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses, signaling a product innovation partnership.
    *   **2025-02-12 (10-K):** Announced agreement to acquire Frontier Communications to enhance fiber broadband footprint.
*   **Market & Competitive Position:**
    *   **2025-09-18:** Publicly asserted its position as "America’s Best Wireless Network," citing J.D. Power awards and RootMetrics recognition for network quality and reliability.
    *   **2025-09-24:** Highlighted in a global industry report as a leading player in the growing wireless market.
*   **Capital Allocation & Shareholder Returns:**
    *   **2025-09-28 & 2025-09-16:** Highlighted as a high-dividend stock (yield >6%) with an expected dividend increase in 2025, supported by stable cash flows.
    *   **2025-09-24:** Noted for regaining attention from income investors following a dividend hike and stable 2025 outlook.
*   **Legal & Regulatory:**
    *   **2025-09-11 to 2025-09-17:** Upheld a $46.9 million FCC fine for selling customer location data without consent, after losing an appeal in the Second Circuit.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Legal/Regulatory (Confirmed):** Faces a confirmed material fine ($46.9M) and ongoing legal exposure related to privacy and data sharing practices, as per the court ruling upholding the penalty (2025-09-11/2025-09-17).
    *   **Competition (Confirmed from 10-K):** Operates in a "highly competitive" industry against national carriers (AT&T, T-Mobile), cable MVNOs (Comcast, Charter), and non-traditional competitors (tech companies, LEO satellites) engaged in aggressive pricing and bundling.
    *   **Regulatory Burden (Confirmed from 10-K):** Subject to FCC regulation on spectrum, licensing, and services, creating potential constraints not borne equally by all competitors.
    *   **Technology & Investment (Confirmed from 10-K):** Requires continuous, significant capital investment for network densification, 5G evolution, and fiber expansion to remain competitive.
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Network Leadership (Confirmed):** Claims and is recognized for superior network reliability and performance, a key competitive differentiator (2025-09-18).
    *   **Strategic Bundling (Confirmed):** Uses bundled mobile and home internet offerings to increase customer stickiness and reduce churn (2025-09-15 web context).
    *   **Dividend Profile (Confirmed):** Maintains a strong, growing dividend (>6% yield) that attracts a dedicated investor base, supported by consistent cash flow generation (2025-09-16, 2025-09-28).
    *   **Growth Catalysts (Inference from Strategy):** Positions 5G, broadband (FWA, Fios+Frontier), and AI/enterprise solutions as core strategic growth drivers (2025-08-30, 2025-09-08 web context).

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral.** Verizon's franchise is anchored by a leading network and a large, stable customer base, providing strong cash flows that support a compelling dividend. Recent initiatives in spectrum acquisition, product partnerships (Meta), and the Frontier acquisition signal strategic execution. However, these positives are materially counterbalanced by a confirmed, large regulatory penalty highlighting privacy/legal risk, and an intensely competitive landscape that pressures pricing and necessitates heavy investment. The stock's profile appears best suited for income-focused investors who accept the associated legal and competitive risks.

corrections.issue: Qual report states dividend yield '>6%' and 'supported by consistent cash flows' — the raw quant evidence shows cash flow data is unavailable for the entire lookback period, so the claim of 'stable/consistent cash flows' supporting the dividend cannot be verified from supplied packs. The dividend yield claim is sourced from news articles (2025-09-15, 2025-09-28) but the quant pack does not provide dividend yield or payout data to confirm the >6% figure.
corrections.required_action: Flag that dividend sustainability and yield claims rest on external news sources rather than verified financial data in the supplied packs. Cash flow is explicitly marked as unavailable in the quant evidence.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'ROE has been stable at 0.105' — the sparse valuation history shows ROE was 0.12 on 2024-10-01 and dropped to 0.105 from 2024-11-01 onward. This is a decline from 12% to 10.5%, not simply 'stable.' The report also states net margin range is '0.1..0.1' which appears to be a display artifact for 0.10..0.10 (i.e., 10%), but the actual net_margin values in the sparse history show 0.087 on 2024-10-01 and 0.076 thereafter — a decline, not flat.
corrections.required_action: Correct the characterization: ROE declined from 12% to 10.5% and net margin declined from 8.7% to 7.6% over the window. These are not flat — they represent a meaningful profitability compression that the quant report understates.

corrections.issue: Qual report cites 'improved cash flow' from a 2025-09-15 news article as part of its offset narrative, but the quant pack explicitly states 'Cash flow statements are unavailable for the entire lookback period' and 'Free cash flow generation is therefore not quantifiable from this data.' The qual report also references 'consistent cash flow generation' (2025-09-16, 2025-09-28) as a dividend support — this is sourced from news, not from verified financials.
corrections.required_action: The qual report should note that cash flow claims come from external news commentary and cannot be independently verified from the supplied financial data. This is a material gap given the high leverage (D/E ~2.67x) and the importance of cash flow to debt service and dividend sustainability.

unsupported_claims: Qual report: 'supported by stable cash flows' and 'improved cash flow' — raw quant evidence explicitly states cash flow data is unavailable for the entire lookback period, making this claim unverifiable from supplied packs.
Quant report: 'ROE has been stable at 0.105' — sparse history shows ROE was 0.12 on 2024-10-01, declining to 0.105, representing a ~12.5% decline rather than stability.

Quant report: 'Net margin has stabilized at 7.6% over the last three quarters' — while true for the last three quarters, the sparse history shows net margin was 8.7% on 2024-10-01, so the stabilization is at a lower level than the start of the window, which is omitted.

**Verified points:**
- (none)

**Required corrections:**
- [C1] Qual report states dividend yield '>6%' and 'supported by consistent cash flows' — the raw quant evidence shows cash flow data is unavailable for the entire lookback period, so the claim of 'stable/consistent cash flows' supporting the dividend cannot be verified from supplied packs. The dividend yield claim is sourced from news articles (2025-09-15, 2025-09-28) but the quant pack does not provide dividend yield or payout data to confirm the >6% figure. → Flag that dividend sustainability and yield claims rest on external news sources rather than verified financial data in the supplied packs. Cash flow is explicitly marked as unavailable in the quant evidence.
- [C2] Quant report states 'ROE has been stable at 0.105' — the sparse valuation history shows ROE was 0.12 on 2024-10-01 and dropped to 0.105 from 2024-11-01 onward. This is a decline from 12% to 10.5%, not simply 'stable.' The report also states net margin range is '0.1..0.1' which appears to be a display artifact for 0.10..0.10 (i.e., 10%), but the actual net_margin values in the sparse history show 0.087 on 2024-10-01 and 0.076 thereafter — a decline, not flat. → Correct the characterization: ROE declined from 12% to 10.5% and net margin declined from 8.7% to 7.6% over the window. These are not flat — they represent a meaningful profitability compression that the quant report understates.
- [C3] Qual report cites 'improved cash flow' from a 2025-09-15 news article as part of its offset narrative, but the quant pack explicitly states 'Cash flow statements are unavailable for the entire lookback period' and 'Free cash flow generation is therefore not quantifiable from this data.' The qual report also references 'consistent cash flow generation' (2025-09-16, 2025-09-28) as a dividend support — this is sourced from news, not from verified financials. → The qual report should note that cash flow claims come from external news commentary and cannot be independently verified from the supplied financial data. This is a material gap given the high leverage (D/E ~2.67x) and the importance of cash flow to debt service and dividend sustainability.

**Unsupported claims:**
- Qual report: 'supported by stable cash flows' and 'improved cash flow' — raw quant evidence explicitly states cash flow data is unavailable for the entire lookback period, making this claim unverifiable from supplied packs.
- Quant report: 'ROE has been stable at 0.105' — sparse history shows ROE was 0.12 on 2024-10-01, declining to 0.105, representing a ~12.5% decline rather than stability.
- Quant report: 'Net margin has stabilized at 7.6% over the last three quarters' — while true for the last three quarters, the sparse history shows net margin was 8.7% on 2024-10-01, so the stabilization is at a lower level than the start of the window, which is omitted.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Verizon presents a balanced risk/reward profile with improving earnings (+8.3% YoY EPS growth) and a compelling dividend yield (>6%) providing downside support, but high leverage (D/E ~2.67x), declining profitability margins (net margin compressed from 8.7% to 7.6%), and valuation at the upper end of its recent PE range (18.1) limit meaningful upside. The stock is fairly valued for income investors but offers limited capital appreciation potential over the next year.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Verizon presents a balanced risk/reward profile with improving earnings (+8.3% YoY EPS growth) and a compelling dividend yield (>6%) providing downside support, but high leverage (D/E ~2.67x), declining profitability margins (net margin compressed from 8.7% to 7.6%), and valuation at the upper end of its recent PE range (18.1) limit meaningful upside. The stock is fairly valued for income investors but offers limited capital appreciation potential over the next year.
key_factors: Earnings recovery with YoY EPS growth of +8.3% (Q2 2025 EPS $1.18) and TTM net income of $18.22B, though profitability margins have compressed from 8.7% to 7.6% net margin over the past year
High financial leverage with D/E ratio of ~2.67x ($278.92B liabilities vs $104.36B equity), though equity has improved by +$10.56B from Q4 2023 low
Valuation stretched at PE 18.1 (near top of 14.9-19.1 one-year range) despite annual price return of -1.5%, suggesting limited multiple expansion potential

Strategic catalysts including Frontier Communications acquisition (announced 2025-02-12) and EchoStar spectrum talks (2025-09-30) to enhance fiber and 5G positioning, offset by confirmed $46.9M FCC fine and intense competitive pressure

SHW.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: SHW (Decision Date 2025-10-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** TTM revenue is $23.08B. The latest Q2 2025 shows revenue of $6.31B, up +19.0% QoQ from $5.31B in Q1 2025, but nearly flat YoY at +0.7% (vs. $6.27B in Q2 2024). TTM net income is $2.54B. Q2 2025 EPS is $3.00, up +50.0% QoQ but down -14.3% YoY (vs. $3.50).
*   **Margins:** Latest gross margin is 48.9% ($3.12B/$6.31B). Operating margin is 15.6% ($985.7M/$6.31B). Net margin is 11.0% ($754.7M/$6.31B).
*   **Cash Flow:** Unavailable for all periods.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total assets grew from $22.95B (Q4 2023) to $25.36B (Q2 2025). Total equity increased from $3.72B to $4.40B over the same period. Total debt was $8.98B as of Q2 2025, a decrease from $9.48B in Q4 2023.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Mixed signals. Q4 2024 operating income was -$166.6M while net income was $480.1M, indicating possible non-operating gains. YoY EPS and net income growth are negative (-14.3% and -15.2% respectively), while revenue is flat, suggesting margin pressure. Cash flow data to assess accruals is unavailable.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Leverage is significant. Debt-to-equity is 2.04x. Total equity ($4.40B) represents 17.4% of total assets, a modest improvement from prior periods (e.g., 16.2% in Q4 2023). The consistent equity buildup is a positive factor.

**3. Valuation Versus History**
*   Latest P/E diluted is 34.19, within its 12-month range of 29.7 - 39.7.
*   Latest P/B is 19.77, at the lower end of its 12-month range of 18.9 - 26.4.
*   The P/E multiple has compressed from 38.5 in October 2024 to 34.2 in September 2025, coinciding with the period of flat revenue and declining YoY earnings.

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
*   **Latest Price:** $346.26 (Sep 30, 2025).
*   **Range:** 52-week high of $400.42, low of $308.84.
*   **Returns:** The stock price is down -9.8% over the past year and -2.5% over the most recent quarter.
*   **Fundamental Context:** The annual price decline (-9.8%) aligns with the period of negative YoY earnings growth (-14.3% in the latest quarter) and flat revenue, despite stable margins. The recent QoQ earnings recovery has not yet reversed the annual price trend.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** 1) QoQ momentum is strong (revenue +19.0%, EPS +50.0%). 2) Margins are historically robust (gross margin ~49%). 3) The P/B ratio is near the low end of its range (19.77 vs. 26.4 peak).
*   **Downside:** 1) YoY growth has stalled and turned negative for earnings. 2) Leverage is high (Debt/Equity 2.04x). 3) A single quarter of negative operating income (-$166.6M in Q4 2024) within the timeframe reveals potential for volatility. 4) No cash flow data exists to confirm earnings quality.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The fundamental picture is one of stable, high-margin operations facing a period of slowing growth. Valuation is within its historical range but has compressed, reflecting the earnings trajectory. The balance sheet is leveraged but stable. The quantitative risk centers on the persistence of flat/declining YoY earnings and the absence of cash flow validation. The potential for a continued QoQ recovery represents the primary upside. Given the available numbers, the risk/reward appears **balanced to moderately cautious**, contingent on a durable return to positive YoY earnings growth.
SHW.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Analysis: Sherwin-Williams (SHW)**
**Decision Date: 2025-10-01**

### 1. Business/Franchise Read (from 2025-02-20 10-K)
Sherwin-Williams is a dominant global manufacturer and retailer of paints, coatings, and related products. Its business is structured into three core segments:
*   **Paint Stores Group:** Operates 4,773 company-owned stores across the US, Canada, and the Caribbean, serving professional contractors and DIY customers. This is a key revenue and service channel.
*   **Consumer Brands Group:** Produces and distributes branded and private-label products to third-party retailers (e.g., home centers). It also operates 334 stores in Latin America and manages significant intersegment transfers.
*   **Performance Coatings Group:** Sells industrial, automotive refinish, and protective coatings globally through its own branches, sales staff, and distributors.

The company leverages a powerful portfolio of recognized brands (e.g., Sherwin-Williams®, Valspar®, Minwax®) and maintains a large integrated manufacturing and distribution network. Competition exists across all segments from local to international players, with key factors including product quality, innovation, and service.

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated, Pre-2025-10-01)
*   **Corporate Action & Capital Allocation (2025-09-10):** The company announced a repurchase of 1.45 million shares in Q2, framed by analysts as a signal of confidence in long-term value despite softer earnings guidance.
*   **Strategic Acquisition (2025-08-17):** The Suvinil acquisition, expected to close in late 2025, is identified as a key growth catalyst, providing entry into Brazil's home renovation market projected to grow at a 4.5% CAGR through 2030.
*   **Market Narrative & Valuation (2025-09-28):** The "most widely followed narrative" cited by a financial analysis site suggests SHW is undervalued by nearly 10% based on long-term earnings potential and forecasted growth.
*   **Dividend Status (2025-08-23):** SHW was highlighted as a top dividend stock within its sector in a broad market analysis.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets (from 2025-02-20 10-K)
*   **Risk: Raw Material Costs & Supply.** The business is heavily dependent on petrochemical-derived raw materials. Price volatility and supply chain disruptions can pressure margins.
    *   *Offset:* The company mitigates this through inventory management, supplier relationships, alternative sourcing, and internal manufacturing of some inputs.
*   **Risk: Cyclical Demand & Economic Sensitivity.** Performance is tied to industrial activity, construction, and home improvement. Economic downturns can alter sales patterns and reduce demand.
    *   *Offset:* A broad geographic and end-market footprint provides some diversification.
*   **Risk: Intense Competition.** Each segment faces competition from numerous local, regional, and global competitors, which can impact pricing and market share.
    *   *Offset:* The company's scale, brand recognition, product innovation, and service model are cited as competitive advantages.
*   **Risk: Integration & Expansion.** Successfully integrating acquisitions (like the pending Suvinil deal) and expanding operations in foreign markets carry operational and financial risks.
    *   *Offset:* The company has a history of acquisitions and existing operations in Latin America, Europe, and Asia.
*   **Risk: Litigation & Regulation.** The company is subject to environmental, health, and safety regulations and potential liabilities (including those related to historical lead paint), as well as general business litigation.

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral to Cautiously Positive.** Sherwin-Williams possesses a strong, resilient franchise with leading market positions and iconic brands. Recent actions, including a strategic acquisition to bolster its Latin American portfolio and a share repurchase signaling management confidence, are positive qualitative factors. However, the core business is exposed to cyclical industrial and construction demand, and margin pressure from raw material costs remains a persistent operational risk. The market narrative of undervaluation is not confirmed by the fundamental evidence provided. Overall, the confirmed strengths of the business model are balanced against its inherent cyclical and cost-related vulnerabilities.

corrections.issue: The qualitative report states the Suvinil acquisition is 'expected to close in late 2025' and cites a '4.5% CAGR through 2030' for Brazil's home renovation market. The raw qualitative evidence (2025-08-17 ainvest.com article) does confirm these figures, so the claim is supported. However, the qual report frames this as a 'key growth catalyst' without noting that the acquisition has not yet closed and integration risks are acknowledged only generically. This is a minor framing issue, not an unsupported claim.
corrections.required_action: No action required; the claim is sourced from the supplied evidence pack.

corrections.issue: The quantitative report states 'Latest gross margin is 48.9% ($3.12B/$6.31B)' and 'Operating margin is 15.6% ($985.7M/$6.31B).' The raw evidence shows gross_margin=0.489 and operating_margin=0.121. The gross margin figure matches (48.9%). However, the quant report calculates operating margin as 15.6% ($985.7M/$6.31B), while the raw valuation ratios show operating_margin=0.121 (12.1%). The discrepancy arises because the quant report divides Q2 operating income by Q2 revenue, yielding ~15.6%, while the raw pack's operating_margin=0.121 likely reflects a TTM or trailing basis. The quant report should clarify which period the margin refers to, or note the discrepancy.

corrections.issue: The quantitative report states 'The P/E multiple has compressed from 38.5 in October 2024 to 34.2 in September 2025.' The raw evidence confirms pe_diluted=38.5 on 2024-10-01 and pe_diluted=34.192 on 2025-09-30. This is accurate. No issue.

The qual report's risk section (raw material costs, cyclical demand, competition, integration, litigation) is drawn entirely from the 10-K filing, which is appropriate. However, the absence of any SHW-specific external risk signal in the web context pack means the risk assessment relies solely on the 10-K boilerplate. This is not an error but a limitation the PM should note.

: The qualitative report states 'The market narrative of undervaluation is not confirmed by the fundamental evidence provided.' This is a fair characterization — the 2025-09-28 simplywall.st article claims ~10% undervaluation, but the quant pack shows P/E at 34.2 (mid-range) and P/B at 19.8 (low end of range). The qual report appropriately flags this as unconfirmed.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Sherwin-Williams is a high-quality franchise with dominant distribution and iconic brands, but the risk/reward is balanced: valuation has compressed to the lower end of its historical range (P/B 19.8x near the 12-month low of 18.9x), yet the balance sheet remains highly leveraged (D/E 2.04x), YoY earnings growth is negative (-14.3%), and revenue is essentially flat (+0.7% YoY). pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Sherwin-Williams is a high-quality franchise with dominant distribution and iconic brands, but the risk/reward is balanced: valuation has compressed to the lower end of its historical range (P/B 19.8x near the 12-month low of 18.9x), yet the balance sheet remains highly leveraged (D/E 2.04x), YoY earnings growth is negative (-14.3%), and revenue is essentially flat (+0.7% YoY). The QoQ recovery is encouraging but insufficient to offset the structural headwinds.
key_factors: Valuation near cycle lows: P/B of 19.77x is at the low end of its 12-month range (18.9–26.4x), and P/E of 34.2x has compressed from 38.5x a year ago, reflecting the earnings decline but potentially offering a floor.
High leverage persists: Debt-to-equity of 2.04x with $8.98B total debt against $4.40B equity constrains financial flexibility and amplifies downside risk if demand weakens further.
YoY earnings deterioration: Q2 2025 EPS of $3.00 is down -14.3% YoY despite flat revenue (+0.7% YoY), indicating margin pressure; the QoQ recovery (+50% QoQ) is encouraging but only one quarter.
Suvinil acquisition (expected late 2025) offers long-term growth into Brazil's 4.5% CAGR home renovation market, but integration risk and the deal's pending status add near-term uncertainty.

Item 1. Business
Our Company
NVIDIA pioneered accelerated computing to help solve the most challenging computational problems. NVIDIA is now a full-stack computing infrastructure company with data-center-scale offerings that are reshaping industry.
Our full-stack includes the foundational CUDA programming model that runs on all NVIDIA GPUs, as well as hundr
…[truncated 21658 chars]…
 user experience for consumer entertainment and professional visualization applications and create new virtual world and simulation capabilities. Our technologies are instrumental in driving the gaming, design, and creative industries forward, as developers leverage our libraries and algorithms to deliver an optimized experience on our GeForce and NVIDIA RTX platforms. Our computer graphics platforms leverage AI end-to-end, from the developer tools and cloud services to the Tensor Cores included in all RTX-class GPUs. For example, NVIDIA Avatar Cloud Engine, or ACE, is
7
Table of Contents
a suite of technologies that help developers bring digital avatars to life with generative AI, running in the cloud or locally on the PC. GeForce Experience enhances each gamer's experience by optimizing their PC's settings, as well as enabling the recording and sharing of gameplay. Our Studio drivers enhance and accelerate a number of popular creative applications. Omniverse is real-time 3D design collaboration and virtual world simulation software that empowers artists, designers, and creators to connect and collaborate in leading design applications. We also enable interactive graphics applications - such as games, movie and photo editing and design software - to be accessed by almost any device, almost anywhere, through our cloud platforms such as vGPU for enterprise and GeForce NOW for gaming.
Advancing the leading autonomous vehicle platform.
We believe the advent of autonomous vehicles, or AV, and electric vehicles, or EV, is revolutionizing the transportation industry. The algorithms required for autonomous driving - such as perception, localization, and planning - are too complex for legacy hand-coded approaches and will use multiple neural networks instead. Therefore, we provide an AI-based hardware and software solution, designed and implemented from the ground up based on automotive safety standards, for the AV and EV market under the DRIVE brand, which we are bringing to market through our partnerships with automotive OEMs, tier-1 suppliers, and start-ups. Our AV solution also includes the GPU-based hardware required to train the neural networks before their in-vehicle deployment, as well as to re-simulate their operation prior to any over-the-air software updates. We believe our comprehensive, top-to-bottom and end-to-end approach will enable the transportation industry to solve the complex problems arising from the shift to autonomous driving.
Leveraging our intellectual property, or IP.
We believe our IP is a valuable asset that can be accessed by our customers and partners through license and development agreements when they desire to build such capabilities directly into their own products or have us do so through a custom development. Such license and development arrangements can further enhance the reach of our technology.
Sales and Marketing
Our worldwide sales and marketing strategy is key to achieving our objective of providing markets with our high-performance and efficient computing platforms and software. Our sales and marketing teams, located across our global markets, work closely with customers and various industry ecosystems through our partner network. Our partner network incorporates global, regional and specialized CSPs, OEMs, ODMs, ISVs, global system integrators, add-in board manufacturers, or AIBs, distributors, automotive manufacturers and tier-1 automotive suppliers, and other ecosystem participants.
Members of our sales team have technical expertise and product and industry knowledge. We also employ a team of application engineers and solution architects to provide pre-sales assistance to our partner network in designing, testing, and qualifying system designs that incorporate our platforms. For example, our solution architects work with CSPs to provide pre-sales assistance to enable our customers to optimize their hardware and software infrastructure for generative AI and LLM training and deployment. They also work with foundation model and enterprise software developers to enable our customers to optimize the training and fine-tuning of their models and services, and with enterprise end-users, often in collaboration with their global system integrator of choice, to fine-tune models and build AI applications. We believe that the depth and quality of our design support are key to improving our partner network's time-to-market, maintaining a high level of customer satisfaction, and fostering relationships that encourage our customers and partner network to use the next generation of our products within each platform.
To encourage the development of applications optimized for our platforms and software, we seek to establish and maintain strong relationships in the software development community. Engineering and marketing personnel engage with key software developers to promote and discuss our platforms, as well as to ascertain individual product requirements and solve technical problems. Our developer program supports the development of AI frameworks, SDKs, and APIs for software applications and game titles that are optimized for our platforms. Our Deep Learning Institute provides in-person and online training for developers in industries and organizations around the world to build AI and accelerated computing applications that leverage our platforms.
Seasonality
Our computing platforms serve a diverse set of markets such as data centers, gaming, professional visualization, and automotive. Our desktop gaming products typically see stronger revenue in the second half of our fiscal year. Historical seasonality trends may not repeat.
Manufacturing
We utilize a fabless and contracting manufacturing strategy, whereby we employ and partner with key suppliers for all phases of the manufacturing process, including wafer fabrication, assembly, testing, and packaging. We use the expertise of industry-leading suppliers that are certified by the International Organization for Standardization in such areas as fabrication, assembly, quality control and assurance, reliability, and testing. Additionally, we can avoid many of the significant costs and risks associated with owning and operating manufacturing operations. While we may directly procure certain raw materials used in the production of our products, such as memory, substrates, and a variety of components, our suppliers are responsible for procurement of most raw materials used in the production of our products. As a result, we can focus our resources on product design, quality assurance, marketing, and customer support. In periods
8
Table of Contents
of growth, we may place non-cancellable inventory orders for certain product components in advance of our historical lead times, pay premiums, or provide deposits to secure future supply and capacity and may need to continue to do so.
We have expanded our supplier relationships to build redundancy and resilience in our operations to provide long-term manufacturing capacity aligned with growing customer demand. Our supply chain is mainly concentrated in the Asia-Pacific region. We utilize foundries, such as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited, or TSMC, and Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd., or Samsung, to produce our semiconductor wafers. We purchase memory from SK Hynix Inc., Micron Technology, Inc., and Samsung. We utilize CoWoS technology for semiconductor packaging. We engage with independent subcontractors and contract manufacturers such as Hon Hai Precision Industry Co., Ltd., Wistron Corporation, and Fabrinet to perform assembly, testing and packaging of our final products.

### Quantitative Report: NVDA (Decision Date 2025-10-01)

#### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory
- **Revenue**: Latest Q2 (2025-07-27) revenue=46.74B, with QoQ growth of +6.1% and YoY growth of +55.6%. Trajectory shows increase from 18.12B (Q3 2023) to 46.74B (Q2 2025).
- **Earnings**: Latest Q2 net income=26.42B, eps_diluted=1.08. QoQ eps growth +42.1%, YoY eps growth +61.2%. Historical eps varied, e.g., 5.98 in Q1 2024.
- **Margins**: Gross profit=33.85B in Q2 2025, implying gross margin ~72.4% (33.85B/46.74B). Operating income=28.44B, operating margin ~60.8%. Valuation ratios report gross_margin=0.698, operating_margin=0.581, net_margin=0.524.
- **Cash-Flow**: Data unavailable for all periods.
- **Balance-Sheet**: Latest total equity=100.13B, total debt=8.47B. Equity grew from 33.27B (Q3 2023) to 100.13B (Q2 2025). Debt-to-equity ratio=0.085.

#### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience
- **Earnings Quality**: Operating income closely tracks net income (28.44B vs. 26.42B in Q2 2025), suggesting minimal non-operating adjustments. YoY net income growth +59.2%. Cash-flow data unavailable, limiting direct assessment of cash conversion.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Low debt-to-equity of 0.085, with total liabilities of 40.61B against equity of 100.13B. Equity growth indicates strong internal generation.

#### 3. Valuation Versus History
- Latest valuation ratios (2025-09-30): pe_diluted=52.856, p_b=45.712, earnings_yield=0.019.
- Historical range (2024-10-01 to 2025-09-30): pe_diluted range 32.1 to 70.0, p_b range 29.5 to 63.8. Current PE is within the range but above the low of 32.1. From sparse history, PE was as low as 34.52 (2025-04-17) and as high as 68.034 (2024-12-04).
- Net margin range 0.5 to 0.6, current 0.524; ROE range ~0.9, current 0.865 (slightly lower).

#### 4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context
- Price level: close=186.58 on 2025-09-30, near the 52-week high of 187.35.
- Quarterly return: +21.7% (from 2025-07-01 close=153.30).
- Annual return: +50.4% (from 2024-09-26 close=124.04).
- Fundamental alignment: YoY eps growth +61.2% vs. price growth +50.4%, indicating PE compression from historical highs (e.g., PE of 68.034 in Dec 2024 to 52.856 currently).

#### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside
- **Upside**: Sustained revenue and earnings growth (YoY revenue +55.6%, eps +61.2%), high operating margins (operating_margin=0.581), and strong balance sheet (debt-to-equity=0.085) could support valuation expansion if growth persists.
- **Downside**: Elevated valuation (pe_diluted=52.856 vs. historical low of 32.1) leaves room for multiple contraction if growth decelerates. Lack of cash-flow data introduces uncertainty on cash generation.

#### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion
Quantitative evidence shows robust growth and financial resilience, but valuation remains moderately high relative to its range. Price momentum (+50.4% annual) has lagged earnings growth, resulting in PE compression. The risk is centered on valuation re-rating downward if future growth does not meet expectations, while reward depends on continued high growth and margin stability.
NVDA.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment: NVDA (Decision Date: 2025-10-01)**  

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 10-K, 2025-02-26):**  
NVIDIA is a full-stack computing infrastructure company with a dominant platform in accelerated computing, centered on its CUDA programming model and GPU architecture. It operates across four key markets: Data Center (AI, HPC, networking), Gaming, Professional Visualization, and Automotive. The company’s moat derives from its integrated hardware-software ecosystem, which includes over 5.9 million developers and support for 4,400+ applications. Its fabless manufacturing model relies on key suppliers (TSMC, Samsung) and supply chain concentration in Asia-Pacific. NVIDIA’s strategy focuses on advancing its accelerated computing platform, extending AI and graphics leadership, and developing autonomous vehicle solutions.

**2. Dated Recent Developments (from supplied text):**  
- **2025-09-30:** Tech giants are projected to invest over $490 billion in AI infrastructure by 2026, supporting long-term demand for NVIDIA’s platforms.  
- **2025-09-30:** Huawei plans to double production of its Ascend 910C AI chips to 600,000 units in 2026, intensifying competition in China with government subsidies.  
- **2025-09-30:** CoreWeave secured a $14.2 billion AI infrastructure deal with Meta and a $6.5 billion expansion with OpenAI, indicating robust demand for AI infrastructure (beneficial to NVIDIA as a key supplier).  
- **2025-09-15:** Chinese regulators accused NVIDIA of violating anti-monopoly laws, leading to a stock decline.  
- **2025-09-10:** NVIDIA unveiled its Rubin CPX AI chip, contributing to a 4.9% stock rise.  
- **2025-09-02:** NVIDIA warned of legal risks related to missing U.S. regulations for a 15% AI chips fee, stating it cannot make payments due to legal exposure.  
- **2025-09-03:** The Blackwell architecture drove a 17% sequential revenue increase in Q2, with management emphasizing accelerated ramp-up of Blackwell Ultimate.  
- **2025-08-27:** A lawsuit was filed against certain officers and directors for breach of fiduciary duty, unjust enrichment, and other violations, noted in the Q2 10-Q.  

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets:**  
*Risks:*  
- **Regulatory/Legal:** Anti-monopoly accusations in China (2025-09-15) and legal risks from U.S. chip fee regulations (2025-09-02) could disrupt operations and incur costs.  
- **Competitive:** Huawei’s expanding AI chip production in China (2025-09-30) may erode NVIDIA’s market share in a key region.  
- **Supply Chain/Geopolitical:** Reliance on Asia-Pacific suppliers (TSMC, Samsung) and potential mineral export restrictions from China (2025-09-30) pose sourcing risks.  
- **Customer Concentration:** Heavy dependence on large cloud and tech companies (e.g., Meta, OpenAI) ties growth to their investment cycles.  
- **Litigation:** Pending lawsuit against officers/directors (2025-08-27) could distract management and impose liabilities.  

*Offsets:*  
- **Ecosystem Moat:** CUDA’s 5.9 million developers and extensive application support (10-K) create high switching costs.  
- **Innovation Pipeline:** Continuous product launches (Blackwell, Rubin CPX) and full-stack integration maintain technological leadership.  
- **Demand Tailwinds:** Projected $3–4 trillion AI infrastructure market (2025-09-26) and massive tech capex ($490 billion by 2026) underpin growth.  
- **Customer Diversification:** Partnerships across cloud, automotive, and enterprises mitigate single-customer risk.  

**4. Qualitative Assessment:**  
**Positive.** NVIDIA’s franchise remains strong, with an entrenched ecosystem, relentless innovation, and sustained demand from the AI infrastructure build-out. Recent developments confirm leadership in AI hardware/software and expanding customer commitments. However, regulatory and competitive pressures in China, supply chain vulnerabilities, and legal risks present material headwinds that require monitoring. The net qualitative picture favors continued dominance, but with elevated risk factors.

The report does not reconcile this discrepancy, which likely stems from different definitions (e.g., segment-level vs. consolidated, or rounding). → Note the discrepancy between the computed 72.4% and the reported 69.8% gross margin, and clarify which figure is authoritative or explain the difference.
- [C3] Qual report states 'CoreWeave secured a $14.2 billion AI infrastructure deal with Meta and a $6.5 billion expansion with OpenAI, indicating robust demand for AI infrastructure (beneficial to NVIDIA as a key supplier).' The raw evidence confirms the CoreWeave deals but does not explicitly state that NVIDIA is a key supplier to CoreWeave or that these deals are directly beneficial to NVIDIA — this is an inference not grounded in the supplied text. → Qualify the CoreWeave claim as an inference (NVIDIA is widely known as a GPU supplier to CoreWeave, but the supplied evidence does not explicitly confirm this link).

**Unsupported claims:**
- The quant report's claim 'Historical eps varied, e.g., 5.98 in Q1 2024' mislabels the period — the raw pack shows eps=5.98 for period_end=2024-04-28, which is Q1 FY2025, not Q1 2024.
- The quant report computes gross margin as ~72.4% (33.85B/46.74B) while the valuation ratios pack reports gross_margin=0.698; the report does not reconcile these two figures.
- The qual report infers CoreWeave deals are 'beneficial to NVIDIA as a key supplier' — the supplied evidence does not explicitly state NVIDIA supplies CoreWeave.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: NVIDIA's Q2 FY2026 results demonstrate reaccelerating growth (+55.6% YoY revenue, +61.2% YoY EPS) with industry-leading margins (gross ~70%, operating ~58%) and a fortress balance sheet (0.085 debt/equity). While the stock trades near its 52-week high at a P/E of ~53x, the valuation has actually compressed from its December 2024 peak of ~68x as earnings growth has outpaced price appreciation, and the massive AI infrastructure buildout ($490B+ in projected tech capex by 2026) provides a durable demand runway that justifies a premium multiple.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: NVIDIA's Q2 FY2026 results demonstrate reaccelerating growth (+55.6% YoY revenue, +61.2% YoY EPS) with industry-leading margins (gross ~70%, operating ~58%) and a fortress balance sheet (0.085 debt/equity). While the stock trades near its 52-week high at a P/E of ~53x, the valuation has actually compressed from its December 2024 peak of ~68x as earnings growth has outpaced price appreciation, and the massive AI infrastructure buildout ($490B+ in projected tech capex by 2026) provides a durable demand runway that justifies a premium multiple.
key_factors: Revenue growth reaccelerated to +55.6% YoY ($46.74B in Q2 FY2026) with EPS growth of +61.2% YoY, reversing the deceleration concern from prior calls and demonstrating sustained demand momentum.

P/E has compressed from ~68x (Dec 2024) to ~53x (Sep 2025) despite continued growth, as earnings have outpaced the +50.4% annual price return — the stock is cheaper on a forward basis than it was a year ago.

Balance sheet remains a fortress with debt-to-equity of 0.085 and equity growing from $33.3B to $100.1B over two years, providing ample capacity for R&D investment and navigating regulatory headwinds.

AI infrastructure demand remains robust with $490B+ in projected tech capex by 2026 and major customer deals (CoreWeave/Meta $14.2B, CoreWeave/OpenAI $6.5B), though China regulatory risk (anti-monopoly accusations Sep 2025) and Huawei competition (600K Ascend 910C chips planned for 2026) are material headwinds to monitor.

ITEM 1. BUSINESS
GENERAL
NIKE, Inc. was incorporated in 1967 under the laws of the State of Oregon. As used in this Annual Report on Form 10-K (this "Annual Report"), the terms "we," "us,"
